description:
src/lib.rs:277: 'let raw = secret.value.ok_or(SecretError::NotFound)?'

Trigger: HTTP 200 success from get_secret with a Secret response whose 'value' field is null/absent. This represents an SDK or service-side anomaly (a successfully-retrieved secret object with no value), not 'no such secret'.

Bad behavior: caller distinguishes NotFound from Backend errors to drive 'fetch-then-create' idioms. Mapping the malformed-success case to NotFound will cause callers to try to create a secret that already exists, producing a confusing follow-on error from the put() API.

Fix: map None → SecretError::Backend or SecretError::DecodeFailed with a message like 'azure-kv returned 200 with no secret value field; SDK or service anomaly'. Reserve NotFound for the genuine HTTP 404 case already handled in is_not_found.
title: secret.value == None mapped to NotFound; should be DecodeFailed/Backend
